title: Review this uncommitted diff for bugs, security issues, and code quality. Be concise (max 10 lines).
summary_text: - High: New guidance says quote tweets must be manual-only and never sent by `scheduler` ([CLAUDE.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/CLAUDE.md#L106)), but the live path still auto-posts any body containing an X URL via plain `post_tweet(post_text)` ([scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L482), [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L525)) and the test suite codifies that behavior ([test_quote_tweet.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_quote_tweet.py#L183)). This is an operational mismatch, not just docs drift. - Medium: The updated rule also conflicts with generation policy in [account_profile.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/account_profile.py#L18), which still instructs producing quote RTs by embedding `x.com` URLs in the body and using the platform quote card. That contradiction will cause future drafts/auto-approvals to target the deprecated path. - No direct security issues in the diff itself; the main risk is inconsistent operator behavior from contradictory instructions.
